title: Solve 2-step expressions
description: Practice evaluating 2-step whole number expressions with different operations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**

$3+6\times 10=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To solve, we have to multiply before we add.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}3 +6 \times{10}\\\\
&= 3 + (\blueD{6 \times 10})&{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&= 3 + \blueD{60}&{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=63&\text{Step } 3 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**

$38-6\times 4=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To solve, we have to multiply before we subtract.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}38 -6 \times{4}\\\\
&= 38 - (\blueD{6 \times 4})&{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&= 38 - \blueD{24}&{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=14&\text{Step } 3 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**

$7 + 18\div 6=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To solve, we have to divide before we add.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}7 +18 \div{6}\\\\
&= 7 + (\blueD{18 \div 6})&{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&= 7 + \blueD{3}&{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=10&\text{Step } 3 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**

$45\div5-4=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To solve, we have to divide before we subtract.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}45\div{5}-4\\\\
&= (\blueD{45 \div 5})- 4&{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&= \blueD{9} - 4&{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=5&\text{Step } 3 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**

$28\div7 \times 4=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To solve, we have to divide before we multiply.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}28\div{7}\times4\\\\
&= (\blueD{28 \div 7})\times4&{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&= \blueD{4} \times4&{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=16&\text{Step } 3 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**

$10\times4 \div5=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To solve, we have to multiply before we divide.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}10\times4\div{5}\\\\
&= (\blueD{10 \times4})\div5&{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&= \blueD{40} \div5&{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=8&\text{Step } 3 
\end{align}$
$10\times4\div{5}= 8$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**

$145-70+29=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
To solve, we have to subtract before we add.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}145-70+29\\\\
&=  (\blueD{145 - 70} ) + 29&{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&= \blueD{75} + 29&{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=104&\text{Step } 3 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the first step to evaluate $19+56\div7$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate, we have to divide before we add.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The following is the first step in the expression $19 + \blueD{56\div7}$:

- $\blueD{56\div7}$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The following is the first step in the expression $28 - \blueD{64\div8}$:

- $\blueD{64\div8}$

**What is the first step to evaluate $44-4\times10$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate, we have to multiply before we subtract.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The following is the first step in the expression $44-\blueD{4\times10}$:

**What is the first step to evaluate $55 - 15 + 29$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

The following is the first step in the expression $\blueD{55-15} + 29$:

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate, we have to divide before we subtract.

**What is the first step to evaluate $8\times 5 - 10$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate, we have to multiply before we subtract.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The following is the first step in the expression $\blueD{8\times5}  - 10$:

assessment_items.question_content:
Jin tried to evaluate $5 + 2 \times9$ step-by-step.

$\begin{align}
&5 + 2 \times9\\\\
=&7\times9&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=&63&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}
\end{align}$

**Find Jin's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate $5 + 2 \times9$, we multiply before we add.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

In $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$, Jin added $5+2$. 

Jin should have multiplied $2\times9$ first.
Jin's mistake was in $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Jin did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Bob tried to evaluate $12 + 18 \div3$ step-by-step.

$\begin{align}
&12 + 18 \div3\\\\
=~&30 \div3&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=~&10&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}
\end{align}$

**Find Bob's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate $12 + 18 \div3$, we divide before we add.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

In $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$, Bob added $12 + 18$. 

Bob should have divided $18\div3$ first.
Bob's mistake was in $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Bob did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jo tried to evaluate $14 - 6 \times4$ step-by-step.

$\begin{align}
&14 - 6 \times4\\\\
=~&(14 - 6) \times4&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=~&8\times4&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
=~&32&\purpleC{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Jo's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate $14 - 6 \times4$, we multiply before we subtract.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

In $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$, Jo put parentheses around $(14-6)$. 

Jo should have place the parentheses around $6\times4$ first.
Jo's mistake was in $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Jo did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dave tried to evaluate $55 - 15 + 20$ step-by-step.

$\begin{align}
&55 - 15 + 20\\\\
=~&40+20&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=~&20&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}
\end{align}$

**Find Dave's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate $55 - 15 + 20$, we subtract before we add.

[[☃ explanation 2]]
In $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$, Dave correctly subtracted $55 -15$ to get $40$.
In $\blueD{\text{Step 2}}$, Dave incorrectly added $40 + 20$.
Dave's mistake was in $\blueD{\text{Step 2}}$.

choices.content: Dave did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Halle tried to evaluate $9\times4\div6$ step-by-step.

$\begin{align}
&9\times4\div6\\\\
=~&36\div6&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=~&30&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}
\end{align}$

**Find Halle's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate $9\times4\div6$, we multiply before we divide.

[[☃ explanation 2]]
In $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$, Halle correctly multiplied $9\times4$ to get $36$.
In $\blueD{\text{Step 2}}$, Halle incorrectly divide $36\div6$.
Halle's mistake was in $\blueD{\text{Step 2}}$.

choices.content: Halle did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kit tried to evaluate $35 - 20 \div10$ step-by-step.

$\begin{align}
&35 - 20 \div10\\\\
=~&35 - (20\div10)&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=~&35-2&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
=~&33&\purpleC{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Kit's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate $35 - 20 \div10$, we divide before we subtract.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

In $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$, Kit put parentheses around $(20\div10)$. 

The parentheses helped Kit figure out what two numbers to divide first.
$\blueD{\text{Step }2}$ correctly divided $20 \div 10$ to get  $2$.
$\purpleC{\text{Step }3}$ correctly subtracted $35 -2$ to get $33$.
Kit did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Kit did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sara tried to evaluate $21\div7\times8$ step-by-step.

$\begin{align}
&21\div7\times8\\\\
=~&3\times8&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=~&24&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}
\end{align}$

**Find Sara's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To evaluate $21\div7\times8$, we divide before we multiply.

[[☃ explanation 2]]
In $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$, Sara correctly divided $21\div7$ to get $3$.
$\blueD{\text{Step }2}$ Sara correctly multiplied $3\times8$ to get $24$.
Sara did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Sara did not make a mistake.
